Perfectionism Kills.

Methodical
non-utilitarianism.

Make things up. Make mistakes. Be wrong and enjoy learning. This is all part of the creative process. It’s not about utility, it’s about growth and development. It's hard to slow down and embrace nuance in a world obsessed with short-sighted productivity...

Sound is the source.
Simultaneously ubiquitous and subversive.

I don’t believe in magic. I believe in the unfettered power of sound. Deeply misunderstood and underappreciated, sound permeates every aspect of life. For me, it has been the gateway to learning about visual arts, computer science, electronics, culture, psychology, politics, and, of course, music.

Dreaming in audio visual perception systems and noise patterns, a manifesto:

By default, the mainstream is not conducive to awareness. Subvert mainstream thinking and via both process and aesthetic.

No genres. They are not useful.

Professional quality work can be meaningful, but “buttoned up" is not an aesthetic worth pursuing.

Improvisation is a core tenant of creativity. The unpredictable and fleeting nature is directly tied to the flow of reality.

Always be open to experimentation, even if that means experimenting with embracing mainstream thinking.

The idea of "progress" is irrelevant. Being present and grounded overrides black and white dogmas.

Don't hesitate to throw away or redo work. Also, don't get stuck in a loop where nothing is ever finished.

The act of developing and exploring a creative process is more valuable than any creative output.

Art does not need to be accessible.

There is nothing to work towards. You are already there and have always been there.
